response.justification: The candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with the mandatory requirements of this role. The 5–10 year hands-on C++ requirement is essentially unmet — C++ appears only in a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a university thesis project, while the overwhelming career focus has been on JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and D-lang across web, mobile, and embedded contexts. There is no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking systems experience; a brief role at RIDE Capital involved fintech investment-platform work but not trading or fixed-income infrastructure. SQL is listed as a skill but is not substantiated with meaningful SQL Server or Oracle project history on Windows.
strengths: Broad multi-language engineering background with demonstrated ability to ramp on unfamiliar stacks quickly
Strong CI/CD, DevOps, and infrastructure experience across GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, and trunk-based development
Some financial services domain exposure through the RIDE Capital engagement
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI plus a bachelor thesis project), far below the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
No Windows-platform development background; career is predominantly Linux and web-stack focused
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking systems domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed income domain experience
Strong generalist engineering and DevOps background
